plan.description: This project addresses critically low uptake of colorectal cancer screening in Latvia. Colorectal cancer is the third most common cancer in the world and one of the leading causes of cancer death, so early detection is essential. Automated Patient Engagement and Tracking for Colorectal Cancer Screening in Primary Care (Nr. lzp-2024/1-0454).
plan.title: Automated Patient Engagement and Tracking for Colorectal Cancer Screening in Primary Care

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

extraField.label: Publication Date
extraField.description: If Open Access is selected, please indicate the date that the DMP will be made available on Zenodo.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

description.description: Data Management Plan for the project "Automated Patient Engagement and Tracking for Colorectal Cancer Screening in Primary Care (Nr. lzp-2024/1-0454)".
description.label: Dataset for FLPP project lzp-2024/1-0454

tags.tag: 20502 Public Health, Health Services Research, Social Medicine

descriptionTemplate.label: RSU DMP Template
descriptionTemplate.description: This is the data managament plan for Riga Stradins University researchers

fieldSet.title: What is the purpose of the data collection/generation?
fieldSet.description: Give short description of the purpose of the data collection/generation and its relation to the objectives of the project. Also give short summary of the origin of the data

fieldSet.title: To whom the data might be useful ("data utility")?
fieldSet.description: Describe potential users of this dataset in the future. For example, researchers or students of your university or other research organisations. Research data can be useful also to governmental and non-governmental organisations working in the field, as well as to private sector.

section.description: In this section you state the purpose of the data collection/generation, as well as explain the relation to the objectives of the project. Give a summary of the origin of the data. Outline the data utility: to whom will it be useful!
section.title: Purpose of data

selectData.label: Select the data type from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify type here

fieldSet.title: Type of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
Type of data depends on purpose and collection/generation methods. 
Dataset may consist of both primary and secondary data in some cases, however, secondary data is more re-usable as it has undergone processing and cleaning.

selectData.label: Select data format from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify format here

fieldSet.title: Format of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
The choice of a preferred file format is crucial in order to ensure that your data will be readable also in the future. Some file formats are more likely to allow long-term readability than others are. Such formats are usually:
 - non-proprietary
 - open, with documented international standards
 - using standard character encoding, preferably Unicode (e.g. UTF-8)
 - uncompressed (space permitting)

labelData.label: Numerical value

fieldSet.title: Expected volume of the data
fieldSet.description: Size of data is significant when it comes to calculating necessary storage space, as well as space in the designated repository. Here you are expected to give approximate byte size of the data

labelData.label: If No, please describe, if you have considered re-using any of existing data?

fieldSet.title: Are you re-using this dataset?
fieldSet.description:
Before collection of new data, researchers shall consider possibilities to re-use already prepared data within or outside their institution, thus saving human and financial resources.
If you are planning to use other researchers’ data in your research there are a couple of things that you need to take into consideration, such as copyright of datasets, permissions provided through licenses, ethical aspects for reuse, etc.

referenceTypeData.label: Indicate repository where the data set has been deposited

labelData.label: If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its title
If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its URL
Add persistent identifier of the data set

fieldSet.title: If Yes, please indicate what dataset are you re-using?
fieldSet.description: In this case you have to fill rest of the DMP according to characteristics of the data you are re-using, as well as your plans on documenting and processing the data. If the data is stored in repositories such as Zenodo, you can auto-fill description of this dataset.

section.description: Here you are expected to provide the basic information on types, formats and size of data generated/collected in this dataset. It is important to specify, if you will use already existing dataset (re-using data).
section.title: Data characteristics

selectData.label: Please select the used Data Identifier System(s)

labelData.label: If you chose other, please specify the respective standard identification mechanism here

fieldSet.title: Will data be attributed with standard identification mechanism (e.g. persistent and unique identifiers such as Digital Object Identifiers – DOI)?
fieldSet.description: In case of RSU Dataverse, DOI is automatically allocated upon publishing of the dataset

labelData.label: If you chose option "No", please explain why you don't plan to use any metadata

fieldSet.title: Do you plan to provide metadata for your data?
fieldSet.description: Metadata is a crucial part of data publishing, ensuring discoverability of your dataset

referenceTypeData.label: Select metadata standard from the list

labelData.label: If it's not on the list, please specify the title here
If it's not on the list, please specify URL here
If there are no standards in your discipline, describe what type of metadata will be created and how.

uploadData.label: If desired, upload an image of the used schema

fieldSet.title: Will you use any metadata standard?
fieldSet.description: RSU Dataverse applies DDI metadata standard. In cases of extensive metadata and specific schemes, it is possible also to attach file (for example, .txt) with complementary metadata

labelData.label: If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify title here
If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify URL here

fieldSet.title: Will search keywords be provided that optimize possibilities for re-use?
fieldSet.description: You shall provide descriptive keywords regarding the dataset you are publishing - they can highlight both the contents of the dataset and also the potential applications.

fieldSet.title: Will you provide clear version numbers?
fieldSet.description: RSU Dataverse provides automatic versioning of the dataset.

selectData.label: What type of documentation do you plan to provide?

labelData.label: if you chose "Other", please specify type of documentation here

fieldSet.title: Will you document the dataset?
fieldSet.description: Documenting the dataset is very significant to ensure possibilities for researchers (including you on a later stage) to re-use the dataset.

fieldSet.title: Other relevant remarks
fieldSet.description: In case if dataset consists of many files and has a complex structure, it would be relevant to describe your approach to file organization (including structure of files, file naming etc.)

section.description: In this section you have to explain provisions for description and documentation of your data in order to make them findable and re-usable. For that you have to outline the discoverability of data (metadata provision) and use of standard identification mechanisms (persistent and unique identifiers). You have to specify, if you plan to use any naming conventions for keywords. Will it be possible to clearly see versions of the dataset? Also very important to specify standards used for metadata creation. If there are no standards in your discipline, describe what type of metadata will be created and how. Last but not least, you have to decide what kind of documentation you will provide to your data.
section.title: Metadata and documentation

fieldSet.title: Are the data formats open to software applications and to recombination with different data sets?
fieldSet.description: Please describe what are the expected formats (according to Data Summary) and are they accessible with widely used and/or free software?

labelData.label: If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's title here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's version here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify source's URL here

fieldSet.title: What methods or software tools are needed to access the data?
Is it possible to include the relevant software in the dataset (e.g. source code)?
fieldSet.description: It is important to include source code in cases when researchers are employing very specific or self-made software to collect/generate or process the data. If possible, the source could be deposited also in repositories specifically developed for that (for example, GitHub).

referenceTypeData.label: If yes, please choose from the list

labelData.label: If not on the list, please specify title here
If not on the list, please specify URL here

Describe your choice and how it will make your data understandable and re-usable for other researchers. Will you be using standard vocabulary/taxonomy/ontology for all data types present in your data set, to allow inter-disciplinary interoperability? Describe them! In case it is unavoidable that you use uncommon or generate project specific ontologies or vocabularies, will you provide mappings to more commonly used ontologies?

fieldSet.title: Will you use data standard vocabulary/taxonomy/ontology to make your data interoperable?
Other relevant remarks

section.description: In this section you have to describe how you will handle your data in order to make them interoperable and re-usable.  To make data more accessible and re-usable, you also have to describe how the data will be made available by specifying methods and software tools needed to access and use the data. Significant part is to explain how you will describe the data, will you use any standard vocabulary/taxonomy/ontology.
section.title: Data accessability

referenceTypeData.label: Please choose repository from the list

labelData.label: If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's title here
If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's URL here
Please justify your choice! Have you explored appropriate arrangements with the identified repository?

fieldSet.title: Will data, associated metadata, documentation and code be made accessible with means of a repository?
fieldSet.description: Preference should be given to certified repositories which support open access where possible. In RSU case data shall be deposited in RSU Dataverse, if there are no specific requirements from the funder or other restrictions to size of the dataset or type of dataset

referenceTypeData.label: Please indicate the type of licence you are planning to use

labelData.label: Shortly explain your choice

fieldSet.title: How will the data be licensed to permit the widest re-use possible? Indicate the license planned to use
fieldSet.description: Data in RSU Dataverse can be licences with one of the copyrights licences provided by Creative Commons - https://creativecommons.org/licenses/

visibilityRule.value: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)
restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)
restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

options.label: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)
options.value: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

options.label: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)
options.value: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)

options.label: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)
options.value: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

labelData.label: If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided? If certain datasets cannot be shared or need to be shared under restrictions, explain why. Clearly separate legal and contractual reasons from voluntary restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their data closed if relevant provisions are made in the consortium agreement and are in line with the reasons for opting out

fieldSet.title: What type of access the dataset will have (choose one)?
fieldSet.description: It is important to note that data shall be as open as possible, as closed as necessary, i.e. data or at least parts of the data shall be open to everyone. If there are any specific reasons to restrict access, these should be clearly listed

labelData.label: Please indicate here the last data of embargo period
Please specify the embargo conditions here

fieldSet.title: Will there be an embargo period for access to these data?
fieldSet.description: Embargo period shall be applied only in cases when there are certain research outputs based on this dataset to be published. Thus embargo period shall not exceed time appropriate for publishing the respective research outputs.

labelData.label: If possible, indicate approximate date for that
How long data will remain re-usable for others?

fieldSet.title: When data will be made available for re-use?
fieldSet.description: Here you should specify plans for the date of publishing the dataset in repository (with or without embargo period).

fieldSet.title: Will you provide reference to the dataset in scientific publications and other research outputs?
fieldSet.description: It is significant to provide link to the dataset, as well as data availability statement (if applicable) in research outputs such as scientific publications. In more and more journals it is already mandatory to provide a link to dataset in a trusted repository. It also gives more validity and trustworthiness to the publication. Otherwise, it is possible that public dataset will gain new collaborations

fieldSet.title: Other relevant remarks

section.description: In this section you have to describe plans for publishing this dataset (including information on repository, license), as well as what will be the access to dataset and how it will be accessible to third parties.
section.title: Data publishing and long-term preservation

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please indicate it here
Numerical value

fieldSet.title: Which costs will be covered within this project for making data FAIR?
Have you discussed how the costs will be covered?
fieldSet.description: Will there be costs covered partly also from other sources of funding? Note that you have to check your grant agreement. In RSU Dataverse long term preservation costs are covered by RSU.

labelData.label: Shortly describe distribution of tasks between the institutions

fieldSet.title: Which institutions are responsible for handling the data?

labelData.label: Shortly describe distribution of tasks related to data managament process

fieldSet.title: Who will be responsible for data management in your project?

labelData.label: Explain data quality assurance processes, also after the end of the project, in order to keep data updated and properly available for re-use

fieldSet.title: Are there data quality assurance processes foreseen?

section.description: In this section you have to outline the resources available to create, publish and store your data. Therefore, estimate the costs for making your data FAIR. Describe how you intend to cover these costs. Clearly identify responsibilities for data management in your project and describe quality assurance procedures to keep your data up to date.
section.title: Allocation of resources and quality assurance

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
Describe these data security measures - how are you going to apply them

fieldSet.title: What security measures will be used for data security?

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
Describe these data security provisions, taking into account these shall be applied also before publishing data

fieldSet.title: What provisions are in place for data security?
fieldSet.description: Please describe how you will ensure safety of the data during the research (including raw data).

labelData.label: Describe storage facility/software you will use to storage and analyse data during research. If it is outside the scope of your institution, you shall describe the arrangements with other institutions/facilities regarding data safety.

fieldSet.title: Where the data will be storaged during research?
fieldSet.description: When planning data management, it is crucial to understand how and where data will be storaged, including phases where data is analyzed or pseudonymized.

labelData.label: If yes, describe how the respective repository will ensure long term preservation and curation

fieldSet.title: Have you ensured that your chosen repository is appropriate for long term preservation and curation?
fieldSet.description: RSU Dataverse has all the necessary measures to ensure secure long term preservation and curation

section.description: In this section you have to delineate the provisions and measures to ensure security of your data both during the project and after it.
section.title: Data security

labelData.label: If yes, please describe them here

fieldSet.title: Are there any ethical or legal issues that could have an impact on data collection and sharing?
fieldSet.description: Ethical and legal issues are mostly related to personal data and sensitive information, but it certainly isn't limited to it. You have to consider also commercial aspects, contractual arrangements with the funder, possibility of classified information etc.

fieldSet.title: Have you got/will you get permission from ethics committee to collect and process data (if applicable)?
Is informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation included in questionnaires dealing with personal data (if applicable)?
Will data collected/generated include personal data?
Will data collected/generated include sensitive information?

options.label: Data accompanied by informed consent statements
options.value: data accompanied by informed consent statements

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
If data set will include personal data/sensitive information, describe methods for processing and accessing data (including anonymization/pseudonymisation of data)

fieldSet.title: If yes, what are the methods used to process and access personal data/sensitive information

section.description: In this section you have to describe any ethical or legal issues related to data you plan to publish. You especially have to consider how you prevent access to personal data or that you protect sensitive information. You also have to describe technical methods to process and access personal data or sensitive information. In order to fill this section, it is also significant to review national legislation, as well as contact ethics committee.
section.title: Ethical and legal aspects

field.textValue: While we do not plan to use a formal, domain-specific ontology at this stage, we will ensure interoperability by using clear and consistent terminology within our codebook and documentation, and we will adopt standard field names where applicable, with consideration for potential future alignment with established vocabularies in the healthcare domain.

Potential limitations (ethical and legal aspects) have been identified, but all of these will be taken into account during data collection, processing, and storage. One example of this is the anonymization of data to protect patient personal data.

textListValues.textListValue: data accompanied by informed consent statements

field.textValue: RSU will manage and handle all data.

Data collection for colorectal cancer (CRC) screening will involve gathering information from the implemented tool on patient invitations, visit attendance, and screening outcomes.

The automated tool, implemented in GP practices, will collect data on colorectal cancer screening, including patient invitations, visit attendance, and screening results.

textListValues.textListValue: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

field.textValue: Currently, our dataset has a relatively simple structure. However, we anticipate that the data structure may evolve as the project progresses and the automated screening tool is further developed. We will document any changes to the file organization, including file naming conventions and directory structure, in our project's ReadMe file and data dictionary, ensuring consistent and clear documentation throughout the project lifecycle.
The data will be provided in CSV and/or JSON formats. Both formats are non-proprietary, ensuring accessibility with widely used and free software.

Aggregated data collected from the automated colorectal cancer screening tool will provide valuable insights into the efficiency of the invitation system. By analyzing metrics such as invitation uptake rates, and screening completion percentages, researchers and government agencies can assess the system's performance. This anonymized, summarized data can inform decisions regarding program optimization, resource allocation, and policy adjustments to improve screening participation and ultimately contribute to better public health outcomes in colorectal cancer prevention.
Specific data quality assurance processes will be defined, updated and documented in detail at a later stage of the project.
To access the CSV and JSON data, users can utilize common spreadsheet software, text editors, programming languages like Python or R with relevant libraries, or database management systems.
RSU Dataverse long term preservation costs are covered by RSU.
In the current project scope, there are no plans to re-use other datasets.

During the research phase, operational data generated by the automated screening tool will be stored in a secure database located within Europe, ensuring compliance with relevant data privacy regulations. The best long-term storage solution for the extracted data used for analysis will be determined at a later stage, taking into consideration factors such as data volume, sensitivity, and intended use, with a commitment to following FAIR data principles.
